records.ety: [F. parcelle a small part, fr. (assumed) LL. particella, dim. of L. pars. See Part, n., and cf. Particle.]

defs: A portion of anything taken separately; a fragment of a whole; a part.
quotes: Two parcels of the white of an egg.
The parcels of the nation adopted different forms of self-government.

defs: A part; a portion; a piece; as, a certain piece of land is part and parcel of another piece.
An indiscriminate or indefinite number, measure, or quantity; a collection; a group.
quotes: This youthful parcel Of noble bachelors stand at my disposing.

defs: A number or quantity of things put up together; a bundle; a package; a packet.
quotes: 'Tis like a parcel sent you by the stage.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Parceled (?) or Parcelled; p. pr. & vb. n. Parceling or Parcelling.]

defs: To divide and distribute by parts or portions; -- often with out or into.
quotes: These ghostly kings would parcel out my power.
The broad woodland parceled into farms.

defs: To add a parcel or item to; to itemize.
quotes: That mine own servant should Parcel the sum of my disgraces by Addition of his envy.

defs: To make up into a parcel; as, to parcel a customer's purchases; the machine parcels yarn, wool, etc.
Part or half; in part; partially.
